created_from: arXiv metadata and source/PDF-accessible primary text
commands: rg -n benchmark/Table/MeV/GeV/gauge/FOPT terms in downloaded arXiv sources
warnings: Some exact benchmark values were extracted from arXiv source text rather than reproduced from final journal PDFs.
No FOPT/PTA calculation was performed; benchmark hints are literature-derived only.
Exact same charge convention and backend assumptions were not found for all benchmark sources.
approximations: Mapping of source vev and dark-vector mass conventions to the proposed model is approximate unless the source uses the same scalar charge normalization.
For papers with portals or kinetic mixing, downstream scans should decide whether to keep those couplings fixed to zero or treat the benchmark only as a dark-sector mass/coupling guide.

papers.title: Gravitational waves from a dark U(1)_D phase transition in the light of NANOGrav 12.5 yr data
papers.brief_description: Studies a classically conformal dark U(1)_D extension with a complex scalar and vector-like singlet fermion variant, targeting the NANOGrav 12.5 yr FOPT interpretation.
papers.model_studied: Dark U(1)_D gauge extension with complex scalar Phi, massive Z_D from Coleman-Weinberg breaking, and optional charged singlet fermion for dark matter/neutrino-mass variants.
papers.similarity_to_target: Very close Abelian Coleman-Weinberg dark-sector FOPT target, but the paper neglects the dark Yukawa in the FOPT analysis and uses a vector-like fermion setup rather than the proposed anomaly-safe Weyl pair.
experimental_constraints: BBN/CMB constraints on light dark vectors and thermal sub-GeV dark matter

benchmark_points.label: NANOGrav 12.5 yr benchmark

conventions.yukawa: source takes y negligible for FOPT

benchmark_points.notes: Useful low-mass dark U(1)_D PTA benchmark; adapt because target keeps y_D as a scan parameter.

assumptions: Neglects Higgs portal coupling in the main FOPT analysis.
Takes dark Yukawa negligible compared with gauge coupling.

papers.title: Supercooled phase transitions in conformal dark sectors explain NANOGrav data
papers.brief_description: Presents conformal dark U(1)' models with supercooled MeV-scale FOPTs that fit the NANOGrav 15 yr signal, using PTArcade for likelihood regions.
papers.model_studied: SM plus gauged dark U(1)' and a conformal dark scalar sector with Coleman-Weinberg breaking; includes kinetic mixing/portal structure and RG evolution.
papers.similarity_to_target: Strong match to conformal Abelian dark FOPT/PTA target, but not the same anomaly-safe fermion model and not the same zero-portal/zero-kinetic-mixing backend limit.
experimental_constraints: BBN dark radiation bound through Delta N_eff
Perturbativity and vacuum stability up to 1e8 GeV

benchmark_points.label: Best fit including SMBHBs

conventions.source_parameters: g_L, lambda_sigma, lambda_sigma_h, g12 rather than target g_D,y_D basis
conventions.target_mapping: Use as mass/coupling/FOPT scale guidance, not direct y_D input

benchmark_points.notes: Provides a conformal Abelian PTA benchmark but no direct target-model y_D.

assumptions: Bubble wall velocity set to 1 for supercooled transitions.
Uses latest LISA Cosmology working group templates for bubble collisions and sound waves.
Fixes very small kinetic mixing for MeV-scale thermalization.

papers.title: Sub-GeV dark matter and nano-Hertz gravitational waves from a classically conformal dark sector
papers.brief_description: Studies a classically conformal hidden U(1)' dark sector where a MeV-scale FOPT generates a nano-Hertz PTA signal and a stable fermion obtains sub-GeV dark matter mass.
papers.model_studied: Hidden U(1)' with dark photon, dark Higgs, stable fermionic dark matter, Yukawa coupling, kinetic mixing to the SM, and conformal symmetry breaking.
papers.similarity_to_target: Closest found source for the target ingredients: conformal Abelian Higgs dark sector, dark vector, stable fermion and Yukawa coupling, and PTA/NANOGrav-relevant FOPT. Differences remain in charge normalization and portal/kinetic-mixing assumptions.
experimental_constraints: BBN and CMB constraints on MeV-scale dark sectors
Dark photon laboratory searches and beam-dump constraints

benchmark_points.label: Point A coupled dark sector best fit

conventions.mapping_note: For equal physical vector mass and vev, target g_D is approximately source g/2 if scalar charge differs by factor 2.

benchmark_points.notes: Best direct starting point for downstream scans, modulo charge normalization and kinetic mixing.

benchmark_points.label: Point B secluded dark sector best fit

conventions.mapping_note: Use charge-normalization mapping before comparing to target g_D.

benchmark_points.notes: Useful high-vev/supercooled benchmark; check BBN/reheating and backend validity carefully.

assumptions: Bubble wall velocity is set to 1.
FOPT contribution is fitted jointly with or against SMBH binary background assumptions.
Kinetic mixing is included for laboratory and cosmological constraints, unlike the target backend limit.

papers.title: NANOGrav Signal from a Dark Conformal Phase Transition
papers.brief_description: Explores a nearly conformal confining dark sector phase transition as a NANOGrav signal source.
papers.model_studied: Nearly conformal dark sector with dilaton/radion-like shallow potential and dark Yang-Mills dynamics.
papers.similarity_to_target: Same conformal/PTA theme but different non-Abelian confining mechanism rather than perturbative Abelian Higgs Coleman-Weinberg model.
observables: dark conformal phase transition GW spectrum
assumptions: Dark sector can be thermally decoupled from the visible sector.
Confining phase transition is described by a dilaton/radion potential.

papers.title: Phase transition and vacuum stability in the classically conformal B-L model
papers.brief_description: Studies phase transition and gravitational waves in a classically conformal gauged B-L model.
papers.model_studied: Classically conformal gauged B-L extension of the Standard Model.
papers.similarity_to_target: Useful Abelian conformal and gauge-dependence/vacuum-stability context, but not a dark U(1)_D PTA/NANOGrav benchmark.
assumptions: Metadata-only in this scan; not used for benchmark extraction.

queries: classically conformal U(1) dark sector first order phase transition gravitational waves NANOGrav
Coleman Weinberg U(1) dark photon gravitational waves first order phase transition NANOGrav
Sub-GeV dark matter nano-Hertz gravitational waves classically conformal dark sector
classically conformal U(1) B-L gravitational waves NANOGrav first order phase transition

novelty_verdict: The broad model class is already studied for PTA/NANOGrav FOPT signals; the exact backend-ready target charge convention with anomaly-safe Weyl pair and fixed zero portal/kinetic mixing appears to be a variant rather than a wholly new literature class.
novelty_notes: The closest exact physics ingredients are in arXiv:2502.19478, but it includes kinetic mixing and a specific dark matter setup. arXiv:2105.01007 is very close for conformal dark U(1)_D FOPT and NANOGrav 12.5 yr, but it neglects the Yukawa in the FOPT scan. No exact source was found with the proposed q_Phi=2, q_psi=+/-1 backend basis and simultaneous y_D scan.

conventions.suggested_target_mapping: try g_D approximately 0.34 at v_D=173 MeV for equal vector mass; also scan around g_D=0.3-0.7 because backend conventions may differ

benchmark_hints.notes: Most useful single starting point because it includes conformal U(1), fermion Yukawa, sub-GeV scale, and PTA likelihood.

conventions.suggested_target_mapping: try g_D approximately 0.30 at v_D=692 MeV for equal vector mass

benchmark_hints.notes: High-vev, strongly supercooled benchmark; downstream should check validity and BBN-related reheating constraints.

conventions.source_basis: RG-evolved g_L, M_h2, M_Zprime at mu=0.1 MeV
conventions.target_basis: v_D, g_D, y_D with Coleman-Weinberg beta_lambda

benchmark_hints.notes: Good conformal U(1)' PTA scale guide but no direct y_D mapping.
Useful low-scale check case; target y_D should initially be small or scanned subject to positive beta_lambda.

conventions.model_type: nearly conformal confining dark sector, not Abelian Higgs

benchmark_hints.notes: Use only for qualitative context on conformal dark-sector PTA explanations.

constraint_summary: NANOGrav/PTA preference points toward MeV-scale transition or reheating temperatures and dark-sector masses from tens to hundreds of MeV in closest conformal U(1) sources.
BBN and Delta N_eff constraints are repeatedly important for MeV-scale reheating, dark radiation, and decays of light dark scalars/vectors.

Laboratory dark photon constraints become relevant when kinetic mixing is not set to zero; target backend sets epsilon=0, but later constraint analysis should revisit whether exact zero is technically natural or imposed.
theory_uncertainties: Gauge and renormalization-scheme dependence of effective potentials in Coleman-Weinberg Abelian models.
Convention mismatch in vector mass-over-vev relation across sources.
Bubble wall velocity and efficiency-factor assumptions differ across papers.
Supercooled transitions may require careful percolation/completion and reheating checks.
recommended_next_steps: Use arXiv:2502.19478 Points A and B as primary PTA-scale starting points, after mapping vector mass convention to m_X/v_D=2 g_D.
Run FOPT scans over v_D roughly 20 MeV to 1 GeV, g_D roughly 0.25 to 0.8, and y_D roughly 0 to 0.5 while enforcing positive Coleman-Weinberg beta_lambda.
Include a low-scale cross-check near the arXiv:2105.01007 benchmark g_D=0.32 and v_D approximately 20 MeV.
Have critic-agent check anomaly, Yukawa gauge invariance, and exact charge normalization before downstream numerical scans.
